---
title: "Bayesian Logistic Regression"
author: "Fedor Bentsa, Shawn Lokshin, Borys Kocherev"
date: "2026-04-13"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(lubridate)
library(rstanarm)
library(bayesplot)

options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
```

##loading data

```{r}
movies <- read.csv("tmdb_5000_movies.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

movies <- movies %>%
  select(
    title,
    budget,
    revenue,
    runtime,
    popularity,
    genres,
    release_date,
    original_language,
    vote_average,
    vote_count
  )

movies <- movies %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(budget),
    !is.na(revenue),
    !is.na(runtime),
    !is.na(popularity),
    !is.na(genres),
    !is.na(release_date),
    budget > 0,
    revenue >= 0,
    runtime > 0,
    popularity > 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    success = ifelse(revenue > budget, 1, 0),
    release_date = ymd(release_date),
    release_month = month(release_date),
    is_english = ifelse(original_language == "en", 1, 0),
    log_budget = log(budget),
    log_popularity = log(popularity + 1)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(release_month))

dim(movies)
head(movies[, c("title", "budget", "revenue", "success")])
```

##extracting genre info

```{r}
extract_genres <- function(genre_string) {
  out <- tryCatch({
    if (is.na(genre_string) || genre_string == "") return(character(0))
    parsed <- fromJSON(genre_string)
    if (!is.data.frame(parsed)) return(character(0))
    if (!("name" %in% names(parsed))) return(character(0))
    as.character(parsed$name)
  }, error = function(e) character(0))
  
  return(out)
}

genre_list <- lapply(movies$genres, extract_genres)
all_genres <- unlist(genre_list)
top_genres <- names(sort(table(all_genres), decreasing = TRUE))[1:5]

top_genres
```

##creating genre dummy variables

```{r}
for (g in top_genres) {
  clean_name <- paste0("genre_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", tolower(g)))
  movies[[clean_name]] <- sapply(genre_list, function(x) as.integer(g %in% x))
}

model_data <- movies %>%
  select(
    success,
    log_budget,
    log_popularity,
    runtime,
    release_month,
    is_english,
    starts_with("genre_")
  ) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(
    log_budget = as.numeric(scale(log_budget)),
    log_popularity = as.numeric(scale(log_popularity)),
    runtime = as.numeric(scale(runtime)),
    release_month = as.numeric(scale(release_month))
  )

head(model_data)
```

##splitting into training and testing sets

```{r}
set.seed(4420)

n <- nrow(model_data)
train_index <- sample(1:n, size = floor(0.8 * n))

train_data <- model_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- model_data[-train_index, ]

dim(train_data)
dim(test_data)
```

##tunning prior strength

```{r}
prior_values <- c(2.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0, 0.5)

results <- data.frame(
  prior_sd = numeric(),
  accuracy = numeric()
)

model_list <- list()

for (p in prior_values) {
  
  temp_model <- stan_glm(
    success ~ .,
    data = train_data,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    prior = normal(0, p, autoscale = TRUE),
    prior_intercept = normal(0, 5, autoscale = TRUE),
    chains = 4,
    iter = 2000,
    seed = 4420,
    refresh = 0
  )
  
  posterior_probs <- posterior_linpred(
    temp_model,
    newdata = test_data,
    transform = TRUE
  )
  
  mean_probs <- colMeans(posterior_probs)
  pred_class <- ifelse(mean_probs >= 0.5, 1, 0)
  
  acc <- mean(pred_class == test_data$success)
  
  results <- rbind(
    results,
    data.frame(prior_sd = p, accuracy = acc)
  )
  
  model_list[[as.character(p)]] <- temp_model
}

results
```

##choosing best prior

```{r}
best_prior <- results$prior_sd[which.max(results$accuracy)]
best_prior
```

##fitting final Bayesian logistic regression model

```{r}
bayes_model <- model_list[[as.character(best_prior)]]

summary(bayes_model)
```


```{r}
posterior_interval(bayes_model, prob = 0.95)
```

##final test set predictions

```{r}
posterior_probs <- posterior_linpred(
  bayes_model,
  newdata = test_data,
  transform = TRUE
)

mean_probs <- colMeans(posterior_probs)
pred_class <- ifelse(mean_probs >= 0.5, 1, 0)

accuracy <- mean(pred_class == test_data$success)
cat("Best prior SD:", best_prior, "\n")
cat("Test Accuracy:", round(accuracy, 4), "\n")

conf_mat <- table(
  Predicted = pred_class,
  Actual = test_data$success
)

conf_mat
```

##posterior plot

```{r}
posterior <- as.matrix(bayes_model)

mcmc_areas(
  posterior,
  pars = colnames(posterior)[1:min(8, ncol(posterior))],
  prob = 0.8
)
```
